tweets.text:
🇨🇳 #China Should Focus on More Efficient Computing Power - People’s Daily

➡ China should avoid a rush into oversized computing projects and focus instead on improving how resources are used, arguing that efficiency and practical applications matter more than sheer scale, People’s Daily says in a commentary.

➡ Bigger computing capacity doesn’t automatically mean better results and efficient allocation is what boosts productivity.

➡ AI competition is not just about who has more resources, but who can use them more effectively.

➡ Developing new productive forces should not become costly race to build large projects.

➡ Policymakers should focus on smart planning, innovation and real world applications rather than scale.

*Link (Chinese): https://t.co/5pAM9VgppQ

🇨🇳 #Kimi #K3 achieves a score of 57 on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index, placing it on par with Opus 4.8 and GPT-5.5 - Artificial Analysis

➡ While it trails Fable 5 and GPT-5.6 Sol, #Moonshot AI has announced plans to release the weights for its 2.8 trillion-parameter model, which would make Kimi K3 the most capable open-weights model available.

*Link: https://t.co/qCrzzKWoyu

https://t.co/x0lk8lqdWV
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Big news: Kimi-K3 by @Kimi_Moonshot is now #1 in the Frontend Code Arena with 1679 pts, surpassing Claude Fable 5.

This is a 17-place jump from Kimi-k2.6 (#18 -> #1).

In Frontend, Kimi-K3 ranked #1 in 6 of 7 domains: Brand & Marketing, Reference-Based Design, Data & Analytics, https://t.co/Oa6teaQnWp

tweets.text: The US does so at its own peril if it continues to dismiss the success of Chinese AI labs as merely the result of distillation.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: Why do you think Chinese labs only got there by distilling alone and lack all other methods? Did you know the top researchers in Chinese labs went to the same top university with similar GPAs as the top researchers in OpenAI/anthropic, which are mostly Chinese as well?
tweets.full_text: The US does so at its own peril if it continues to dismiss the success of Chinese AI labs as merely the result of distillation.

tweets.text: The success of Kimi-K3 once again suggests that the moat surrounding frontier AI labs may be far weaker than many investors believe. The rapid commoditization of AI services—driven by the swift catch-up of Chinese AI labs—is posing an increasingly significant challenge to the valuation narrative underpinning US frontier AI companies.
